channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Amazon developing driver eyeglasses to shave seconds off deliveries
item.description: Such directions could shave valuable seconds off each delivery by providing left or right directions off elevators and around obstacles such as gates or aggressive dogs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: AI, cybersecurity drive IT investments and lead skill shortages for 2025
item.description: AI and cybersecurity are driving enterprise tech investments and at the same time exposing the talent gaps that IT teams face.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Salesforce: Latest news and insights
item.description: Salesforce is a vendor of cloud-based software and applications for sales, customer service, marketing automation, ecommerce, analytics, and application development. Based in San Francisco, Calif.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 21 proposed features.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cruise admits to filing false report to sway federal probe into San Francisco crash
item.description: The autonomous car company did not include details about a woman being dragged over 20 feet by one of its self-driving vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Jason Green, The Mercury News

item.title: 18 states sue SEC, Gensler for 'regulatory overreach' on crypto
item.description: Eighteen Republican attorneys general sued the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Chair Gary Gensler on Thursday for allegedly overstepping the agency’s authority in its enforcement actions against the cryptocurrency industry.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Man who stole and laundered roughly $1B in bitcoin is sentenced to 5 years in prison
item.description: A computer expert who stole bitcoin worth billions of dollars at current prices — and then spent years laundering some of the hacked cryptocurrency with help from his wife — has been sentenced to five years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: JetBrains IDEs ease debugging for Kubernetes apps
item.description: JetBrains IDEs, with new 2024.3 edition releases, now provide a logical code structure view alongside the familiar physical code structure view in the Structure tool window.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI is everywhere. How should California schools handle it? Teachers’ different approaches show its potential — and limits
item.description: 'We’re trying to teach the ethical use of these new tools that are just going to keep on growing,' one teacher says.
item.dc:creator: Kristen Taketa, The Mercury News

item.title: FBI probe into Chinese hacking finds 'broad and significant' espionage campaign
item.description: The U.S. government's ongoing probe into China-backed actors' attempts to hack into U.S. telecommunications infrastructure revealed a "broad and significant" espionage campaign targeted at those in government or political work, the FBI announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Why that Google Meet error might be a sneaky hacker trick
item.description: ClickFix, a new scam targeting computer users, is on the rise in the U.S. The scam prompts its targets to click on a link to fix a problem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: EU slaps Meta with a nearly 800 million euro fine for engaging in 'abusive' Marketplace practices
item.description: European Union regulators hit Facebook parent Meta with a fine of nearly 800 million euros on Thursday for what it calls “abusive practices” involving its Marketplace online classified ads business.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FBI, CISA say Chinese hackers breached multiple US telecom providers in targeted attack
item.description: The U.S. government said it is investigating after People's Republic of China hackers targeted commercial telecommunications service providers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Facebook and Instagram users in Europe can opt for less personalized ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Facebook and Instagram users in Europe will get the option to see less personalized ads if they don't want to pay for an ad-free subscription, social media company Meta said Tuesday, bowing to pressure from Brussels over privacy and digital competition concerns.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, PIX 11
